mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to a general decline in equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL remains elevated, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of observations over any 2-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market and impending economic recession, which typically leads to a broad sell-off in equities and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points from its 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:Historical correlation of unemployment spikes with recessions
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises as specified, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such triggered instances, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with an accommodative monetary policy (low FEDFUNDS) indicates strong consumer demand and cheap capital, fostering corporate growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, if S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of cases over any 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, rapidly rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) increase the cost of borrowing for businesses and consumers, compressing corporate earnings and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.75 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: If, during identified monetary tightening cycles, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a healthy and expanding manufacturing sector, indicating robust economic activity that typically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production as a coincident economic indicator
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth consistently fails to correlate positively with S&P 500 5-day returns (Rank IC < 0.01) over any 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A strong monthly increase in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) above a significant threshold indicates robust job creation and economic strength, boosting consumer confidence and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong job growth often precedes market rallies
failure_condition: If PAYEMS change exceeds 200,000, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of cases, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) and a rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a broad economic slowdown and potential recession, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average declines > 10% AND UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Housing and labor are key recession indicators
failure_condition: Despite both conditions being met, if S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment of high inflation, strong GDP growth (GDPC1) can be interpreted as an overheating economy, prompting more aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annual growth > 3% AND CPIAUCSL annual growth > 5%
failure_condition: In high inflation regimes, if strong GDPC1 growth is consistently followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns (Rank IC > 0.03) over any 2-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.
